hypothesis: If a 2B language model is continually pre-trained on MOF-specific literature combined with large PubChem structural datasets and then fine-tuned on MOFid-to-property mappings, it will achieve lower MAE on MOF band gap and gas adsorption predictions than structural baselines (CGCNN, SOAP, MOFormer) and require fewer supervised fine-tuning samples to reach comparable performance because pre-training imparts transferable chemical and MOF-specific knowledge to text-based representations.
